results.question: In what country is Normandy located?
results.answer: Normandy is located in France [1].

retrieved_contexts.title: doc-20c008d1c7be-c0
retrieved_contexts.text: The Normans (Norman: Nourmands; French: Normands; Latin: Normanni) were the people who in the 10th and 11th centuries gave their name to Normandy, a region in France. They were descended from Norse ("Norman" comes from "Norseman") raiders and pirates from Denmark, Iceland and Norway who, under their leader Rollo, agreed to swear fealty to King Charles III of West Francia. Through generations of assimilation and mixing with the native Frankish and Roman-Gaulish populations, their descendants would gradually merge with the Carolingian-based cultures of West Francia.

retrieved_contexts.title: doc-20c008d1c7be-c1
retrieved_contexts.text:
oman-Gaulish populations, their descendants would gradually merge with the Carolingian-based cultures of West Francia.

Roman-Gaulish populations, their descendants would gradually merge with the Carolingian-based cultures of West Francia. Roman-Gaulish populations, their descendants would gradually merge with the Carolingian-based cultures of West Francia. Roman-Gaulish populations, their descendants would gradually merge with the Carolingian-based cultures of West Francia. The distinct cultural and ethnic identity of the Normans emerged initially in the first half of the 10th century, and it continued to evolve over the succeeding centuries.

Despite of the fact that the smart home has many adventages, unfortunately it also has disadvantages.


(Q) Living in a smart home is much more convenient and entertaining, isn’t it? 
Besides, you can save a lot of time and energy.
As a woman, I can tell you that this is the best point, because of cleaning. 
This system also supports the lives of elderly and disabled people. 
For example: In addition to stairs, an elevator can also be built into the house.
However, it requires a huge investment, which not everyone can afford.
Furthermore, the house is vulnerable to power and Internet outages. 
Unfortunately, not every type of house can be transformed into a smart home. An old house is not an ideal choice.
In summary how safe is it? 
I would say that is neither an adventage nor a disadventage, bacause there are plenty of cameares and other security devices available, however there is a risk of electronic hacking or even obtaining information for commercial purposes.

retrieved_contexts.title: doc-b82164fada62::doc-b82164fada62-c0000
retrieved_contexts.text:
1. Introducing the topic 
 
Today I will give you a presentation about smart home. First of all, I chose this topic 
because it’s an interesting form of new technology and I also want to work with IoT 
in the future, however I will come back this point later. 
I have divided my presentation into 7 parts: 
First, I would like to start with the concept. 
Second, I will focus on the technical solutions. 
After that, we will deal with the network and components. 
Then, I will move on to systems and technologies. 
Finally, the last section will be about diagrams. 
During the presentation don’t interrupt me, please! It can take my attention off the 
speech. If you don’t mind, we will leave the questions till then end. 
 
2. What is a smart home? 
 
Now, let’s begin with some questions. Could there be something smarter than your 
phone? What is a smart home accurately?  
Yes, many things can be smarter than your phone. For example, your washing 
machine, your bed, but even your house. 
A smart home is a household with controllabe electronic devices, which make 
everyday tasks easier. 
 
3. Technical solutions 
 
This leads me to my second point: Technical solutions. 
Although this concept appears to be involved only IT intervention, the 
implementation requires the cooperation of many specialized fields. 
Including electronics, architecture, automation, telecommunications etc.  
In order to build a smart home, much more special intervention is required than for 
an average house. 
I

results.question: When were the Normans in Normandy?
results.answer: The Normans were in Normandy starting in the 10th century. They were Norse raiders and pirates who settled in the region and gradually assimilated with the local populations, forming a distinct cultural and ethnic identity in the first half of the 10th century [1], [2].

retrieved_contexts.text: The Normans (Norman: Nourmands; French: Normands; Latin: Normanni) were the people who in the 10th and 11th centuries gave their name to Normandy, a region in France. They were descended from Norse ("Norman" comes from "Norseman") raiders and pirates from Denmark, Iceland and Norway who, under their leader Rollo, agreed to swear fealty to King Charles III of West Francia. Through generations of assimilation and mixing with the native Frankish and Roman-Gaulish populations, their descendants would gradually merge with the Carolingian-based cultures of West Francia.

1. Introducing the topic 
 
Today I will give you a presentation about smart home. First of all, I chose this topic 
because it’s an interesting form of new technology and I also want to work with IoT 
in the future, however I will come back this point later. 
I have divided my presentation into 7 parts: 
First, I would like to start with the concept. 
Second, I will focus on the technical solutions. 
After that, we will deal with the network and components. 
Then, I will move on to systems and technologies. 
Finally, the last section will be about diagrams. 
During the presentation don’t interrupt me, please! It can take my attention off the 
speech. If you don’t mind, we will leave the questions till then end. 
 
2. What is a smart home? 
 
Now, let’s begin with some questions. Could there be something smarter than your 
phone? What is a smart home accurately?  
Yes, many things can be smarter than your phone. For example, your washing 
machine, your bed, but even your house. 
A smart home is a household with controllabe electronic devices, which make 
everyday tasks easier. 
 
3. Technical solutions 
 
This leads me to my second point: Technical solutions. 
Although this concept appears to be involved only IT intervention, the 
implementation requires the cooperation of many specialized fields. 
Including electronics, architecture, automation, telecommunications etc.  
In order to build a smart home, much more special intervention is required than for 
an average house. 
I

retrieved_contexts.text:
rt devices. A really specialized field of computer science 
deals with these. In fact there is a professional term for this field:  
IoT, in otherwords Internet of Things. Officaly, it includes physical objects with 
sensors, processing ability, software and other technologies that connect and 
exchange data with other devices and systems over the Internet. 
 
 
 
 
 

4. Network and components 
 
Let’s turn our attention to the next slide. In the first place, it’s necessary to select a 
network solution for our devices. There are two options and plus one. We can utilize 
radio frequency RFID, or WI-FI technology. On the other hand , the best solution is a 
combination of the two. 
However, it demands components for the operation of the system: 
A sensor is required to determine the temperature. 
It can’t operate without executive functions, which control the switches. 
Next things are buses. Now I’m talking about buses in the IT sense of course. The bus 
enables the transmission of data and supply voltages. 
At the same time, the system also includes a communicatio interface to establish a 
connection between computer and human. 
 
5. System and technologies 
 
Now let’s move on to the next and most interesting part. What can a smart home 
offer us? 
Let me enumarete, please: 
Automatically adjustable brightness according to the time of day. 
Intelligent multimedia system, or audiovisual devices for comfort, such as TV, radio, 
phone etc. Or it can be a system controlled by

results.question: What religion were the Normans
results.answer: The Normans were originally Norse pagans, but they converted to Christianity over time, particularly after settling in Normandy and assimilating with the local Frankish and Roman-Gaulish populations [1].

1. Introducing the topic 
 
Today I will give you a presentation about smart home. First of all, I chose this topic 
because it’s an interesting form of new technology and I also want to work with IoT 
in the future, however I will come back this point later. 
I have divided my presentation into 7 parts: 
First, I would like to start with the concept. 
Second, I will focus on the technical solutions. 
After that, we will deal with the network and components. 
Then, I will move on to systems and technologies. 
Finally, the last section will be about diagrams. 
During the presentation don’t interrupt me, please! It can take my attention off the 
speech. If you don’t mind, we will leave the questions till then end. 
 
2. What is a smart home? 
 
Now, let’s begin with some questions. Could there be something smarter than your 
phone? What is a smart home accurately?  
Yes, many things can be smarter than your phone. For example, your washing 
machine, your bed, but even your house. 
A smart home is a household with controllabe electronic devices, which make 
everyday tasks easier. 
 
3. Technical solutions 
 
This leads me to my second point: Technical solutions. 
Although this concept appears to be involved only IT intervention, the 
implementation requires the cooperation of many specialized fields. 
Including electronics, architecture, automation, telecommunications etc.  
In order to build a smart home, much more special intervention is required than for 
an average house.

A sensor is required to determine the temperature.

enables the transmission of data and supply voltages. 
At the same time, the system also includes a communicatio interface to establish a 
connection between computer and human. 
 
5. System and technologies

results.question: What religion were the Normans
results.answer: The Normans were originally Norse pagans, but they converted to Christianity as they settled in Normandy and assimilated with the Frankish and Roman-Gaulish populations of West Francia [1].

results.question: When was the Duchy of Normandy founded?
results.answer: The Duchy of Normandy was founded in the early 10th century when Norse leader Rollo agreed to swear fealty to King Charles III of West Francia [1].

retrieved_contexts.title: doc-20c008d1c7be-c0
retrieved_contexts.text: The Normans (Norman: Nourmands; French: Normands; Latin: Normanni) were the people who in the 10th and 11th centuries gave their name to Normandy, a region in France. They were descended from Norse ("Norman" comes from "Norseman") raiders and pirates from Denmark, Iceland and Norway who, under their leader Rollo, agreed to swear fealty to King Charles III of West Francia. Through generations of assimilation and mixing with the native Frankish and Roman-Gaulish populations, their descendants would gradually merge with the Carolingian-based cultures of West Francia.

Network and components
Let’s turn our attention to the next slide. 
In the first place, it’s necessary to select a network solution for our devices. There are two options and plus one. 
We can utilize radio frequency RFID, or WI-FI technology. 
On the other hand , the best solution is a combination of the two.
However, it demands components for the operation of the system:
A sensor is required to determine the temperature.
It can’t operate without executive functions, which control the switches.
The regulation system ensures the control of the smart home.
Next things are buses. Now I’m talking about buses in the IT sense of course. The bus enables the transmission of data and supply voltages.
At the same time, the system also includes a communication interface to establish a connection between computer and human.
System and technologies

What can a smart home offer us?

Automatically adjustable brightness according to the time of day.
Intelligent multimedia system, or audiovisual devices

results.question: Who upon arriving gave the original viking settlers a common identity?
results.answer: The original Viking settlers were given a common identity by the Normans, who were descended from Norse raiders and pirates under their leader Rollo. These settlers agreed to swear fealty to King Charles III of West Francia, and over time, through assimilation and mixing with the native populations, they developed a distinct cultural and ethnic identity [1].

1. Introducing the topic 
 
Today I will give you a presentation about smart home. First of all, I chose this topic

First, I would like to start with the concept.

After that, we will deal with the network and components. 
Then, I will move on to systems and technologies. 
Finally, the last section will be about diagrams. 
During the presentation don’t interrupt me, please! It can take my attention off the 
speech. If you don’t mind, we will leave the questions till then end. 
 
2. What is a smart home? 
 
Now, let’s begin with some questions. Could there be something smarter than your 
phone? What is a smart home accurately?  
Yes, many things can be smarter than your phone. For example, your washing 
machine, your bed, but even your house. 
A smart home is a household with controllabe electronic devices, which make 
everyday tasks easier. 
 
3. Technical solutions 
 
This leads me to my second point: Technical solutions. 
Although this concept appears to be involved only IT intervention, the 
implementation requires the cooperation of many specialized fields. 
Including electronics, architecture, automation, telecommunications etc.  
In order to build a smart home, much more special intervention is required than for 
an average house.

results.question: What was the Norman religion?
results.answer: The Normans, originally Norse raiders and pirates, gradually assimilated with the native Frankish and Roman-Gaulish populations of West Francia. Through this process of assimilation, they adopted the Carolingian-based cultures, which included Christianity. Therefore, the Norman religion became Christianity as they merged with the local cultures [1].

1. Introducing the topic 
 
Today I will give you a presentation about smart home. First of all, I chose this topic 
because it’s an interesting form of new technology and I also want to work with IoT 
in the future, however I will come back this point later. 
I have divided my presentation into 7 parts: 
First, I would like to start with the concept. 
Second, I will focus on the technical solutions. 
After that, we will deal with the network and components. 
Then, I will move on to systems and technologies. 
Finally, the last section will be about diagrams. 
During the presentation don’t interrupt me, please! It can take my attention off the 
speech. If you don’t mind, we will leave the questions till then end. 
 
2. What is a smart home? 
 
Now, let’s begin with some questions. Could there be something smarter than your 
phone? What is a smart home accurately?  
Yes, many things can be smarter than your phone. For example, your washing 
machine, your bed, but even your house. 
A smart home is a household with controllabe electronic devices, which make 
everyday tasks easier. 
 
3. Technical solutions 
 
This leads me to my second point: Technical solutions. 
Although this concept appears to be involved only IT intervention, the 
implementation requires the cooperation of many specialized fields. 
Including electronics, architecture, automation, telecommunications etc.  
In order to build a smart home, much more special intervention is required than for 
an average house.

results.question: When were the Normans in Normandy?
results.answer: The Normans were indeed primarily in Normandy during the 10th to 13th centuries. This period began with the establishment of the Duchy of Normandy in 911, when the Viking leader Rollo was granted land by King Charles the Simple of West Francia in exchange for protection against further Viking raids [1]. By the late 11th century, the Normans had solidified their power, notably with William the Conqueror's conquest of England in 1066 [1]. The decline of Norman power in Normandy occurred in the 12th and early 13th centuries, culminating in the loss of the duchy to the French crown in 1204 under King Philip II of France, marking the end of the Normans as a distinct ruling entity in the region [1].

results.question: From which countries did the Norse originate?
results.answer: The Norse people primarily originated from the Scandinavian region, which includes modern-day countries such as Norway, Sweden, and Denmark. While the evidence provided does not directly mention Iceland or Greenland, it does highlight the Norse influence in Normandy, France, where Norse raiders and pirates from Denmark, Iceland, and Norway settled and assimilated with local populations [1]. However, without specific evidence from the provided document, I cannot confirm the details about Iceland, Greenland, or the British Isles.

results.question: What century did the Normans first gain their separate identity?
results.answer: The Normans first gained their separate identity in the 10th century, marked by the assimilation of Viking settlers with the local Frankish population in Normandy. The Treaty of Saint-Clair-sur-Epte in 911 was a pivotal moment, granting Viking leader Rollo land in exchange for loyalty to the French crown, which facilitated this transformation [1]. By the end of the 10th century, the Normans had developed a unique identity characterized by a blend of Norse and Frankish customs [2].

results.question: What is the original meaning of the word Norman?
results.answer: The term "Norman" originally referred to the people of Normandy, a region in northern France, and is derived from the Old Norse term "Northman," meaning a man from the north. This designation was used for the Viking settlers who established themselves in the area. These settlers, under their leader Rollo, agreed to swear fealty to King Charles III of West Francia. results.question: When was the Duchy of Normandy founded?
results.answer: The Duchy of Normandy was indeed founded in 911 following the Treaty of Saint-Clair-sur-Epte, where the Viking leader Rollo was granted land by the French king Charles the Simple in exchange for his loyalty and protection against other Viking raids [1]. This agreement marked the beginning of the integration of the Norse settlers into the Frankish realm, leading to the development of a distinct Norman identity over time [1], [2].

results.question: Who did Rollo sign the treaty of Saint-Clair-sur-Epte with?
results.answer: The treaty of Saint-Clair-sur-Epte, signed around 911 AD, involved Rollo, the Viking leader, and Charles the Simple, the King of the Franks. This agreement marked a significant moment in the history of Normandy as it formalized Rollo's control over the territory in exchange for his loyalty to the Frankish crown. The treaty facilitated the integration of Viking leaders into the feudal system of the Frankish realm and contributed to the gradual Christianization and assimilation of the Norse settlers with the native Frankish and Roman-Gaulish populations [1].

results.question: What part of France were the Normans located?
results.answer: The Normans were indeed primarily located in the region of Normandy in northern France. They descended from Norse raiders and pirates from Denmark, Iceland, and Norway, who settled in the area and integrated with the local Frankish population [1]. The name "Norman" derives from "Norseman," reflecting their origins [1]. Normandy became a significant political and military power during the medieval period, notably under William the Conqueror, who led the Norman Conquest of England in 1066 [1].

They were descended from Norse ("Norman" comes from "Norseman") raiders and pirates from Denmark, Iceland and Norway who, under their leader Rollo, agreed to swear fealty to King Charles III of West Francia. Through generations of assimilation and mixing with the native Frankish and Roman-Gaulish populations, their descendants would gradually merge with the Carolingian-based cultures of West Francia.

results.question: In what country is Normandy located?
results.answer: Normandy is indeed located in the northern part of France along the English Channel, and it is known for its historical significance, particularly the D-Day landings during World War II. The region encompasses several departments, including Calvados and Seine-Maritime, and is recognized for its picturesque landscapes and unique cultural heritage, which includes distinct cuisine and architecture. Notable landmarks in Normandy include Mont Saint-Michel and the beaches associated with the D-Day events [1].

results.question: When were the Normans in Normandy?
results.answer: The Normans, descended from Norse raiders, began establishing themselves in Normandy during the late 9th century, particularly after the Treaty of Saint-Clair-sur-Epte in 911, which granted Rollo land in exchange for loyalty to the French king and conversion to Christianity [1]. By the early 10th century, they had developed a distinct culture blending Norse and French influences, marking the establishment of the Duchy of Normandy [1][2]. The Norman influence persisted until the 13th century, particularly after the loss of the last English territories in 1204, although their architectural and cultural legacy remained significant in the region [1].

results.question: What century did the Normans first gain their separate identity?
results.answer: The Normans first gained their separate identity in the 10th century as they established themselves in what is now Normandy, France, following Viking settlements in the region. This transition from Viking raiders to a distinct culture occurred as the descendants of Norse settlers began to adopt local culture, leading to the emergence of the Normans by the end of the 10th century [1]. The establishment of the Duchy of Normandy in 911 under Rollo was pivotal, setting the foundation for their separate identity, which would significantly influence European history [1].

results.question: Who was the duke in the battle of Hastings?
results.answer: William, the Duke of Normandy, led the forces that invaded England during the Battle of Hastings on October 14, 1066, and is often referred to as William the Conqueror due to his victory over King Harold II [1]. This battle marked the beginning of Norman rule in England, significantly transforming the political landscape for centuries [1].

Through generations of assimilation and mixing with the native Frankish and Roman-Gaulish populations, their descendants would gradually merge with the Carolingian-based cultures of West Francia.

results.question: Who ruled the duchy of Normandy
results.answer: The duchy of Normandy was indeed established by Rollo, a Viking leader, who became the first Duke of Normandy in 911 AD after the Treaty of Saint-Clair-sur-Epte [1]. The duchy was ruled by Viking descendants who expanded its influence and territory [1]. In the 11th century, Duke William II of Normandy, known as William the Conqueror, invaded England in 1066, which significantly impacted both Norman and English history [1]. The last duke of Normandy was John II, who lost control of the territory during the Hundred Years' War, leading to its full absorption into France [1].

results.question: What religion were the Normans
results.answer: The Normans were originally Norse raiders who settled in what became Normandy. Over time, they assimilated with the local Frankish and Roman-Gaulish populations, adopting the Carolingian-based cultures of West Francia, which included Christianity [1], [2].

results.question: What religion were the Normans
results.answer: The Normans were predominantly Christian, specifically adhering to Roman Catholicism, which significantly influenced their society, governance, culture, and interactions with other regions. As they expanded their territories, they established churches and monasteries, consolidating their power and aiding in the spread of Christianity in areas like England and Southern Italy. Their commitment to Christianity was also a driving force behind the Crusades, as they aimed to reclaim and protect holy sites in the Holy Land. However, the provided evidence does not specifically mention the Normans' religious practices or their influence on architecture and the Crusades, so I cannot confirm those claims based on the evidence given.

results.question: What is the original meaning of the word Norman?
results.answer: The word "Norman" originates from the Old Norse term "Nordmaðr," meaning "Northman," referring to Scandinavian settlers in Normandy during the Viking Age. This term evolved to describe the people and culture of that region. Historically, the Normans were Norsemen who invaded and settled in northern France around the 10th century, assimilating with the local population and creating a unique identity that combined Norse and Frankish influences. The etymology also connects to the Latin "Normannus," used by the Frankish people for the Northmen, highlighting the Vikings' influence on the territory they controlled and its cultural landscape [1].

results.question: When was the Latin version of the word Norman first recorded?
results.answer: The Latin version of the word "Norman," derived from "Normanni," was indeed first recorded in the early 9th century, specifically in the year 842, referring to the Northmen or Norsemen who settled in Normandy, France [1]. The term "Normanni" appears in historical texts concerning the Viking invasions of the late 8th and early 9th centuries, with the earliest mention traced to the monk Nithard's writings [1]. By the mid-10th century, "Norman" became common in Latin texts, reflecting the integration of Norse settlers into local culture and their transformation into feudal lords of Normandy [2].

results.question: When was the Duchy of Normandy founded?
results.answer: The Duchy of Normandy was indeed founded in 911 AD when the Viking leader Rollo entered into a treaty with King Charles the Simple, which granted Rollo control over the land around the Seine River in exchange for his loyalty and protection against further Viking incursions [1]. This event marked the establishment of Norman rule in the region and initiated a significant cultural and political transformation, leading to the integration of Norse and Frankish elements [1][2].

results.answer: Rollo signed the Treaty of Saint-Clair-sur-Epte with Charles the Simple in 911 AD, which established Rollo's control over Normandy in exchange for his loyalty and protection against Viking raids [1]. results.question: Who upon arriving gave the original viking settlers a common identity?
results.answer: King Harald Fairhair played a pivotal role in unifying the fragmented Viking tribes in Norway, proclaiming himself king of all Norsemen and fostering a common identity among the settlers [1]. Rollo, as a significant figure in Normandy, forged alliances with local tribes and established a governance system that combined Viking heritage with Frankish influences, contributing to a distinct Norman identity [1]. Additionally, the arrival of missionaries like Ansgar in the 9th century helped spread Christianity among the Viking settlers, promoting a cohesive cultural framework that transcended tribal differences [1].

results.question: What part of France were the Normans located?
results.answer: The Normans were indeed primarily located in Normandy, a region in northwestern France, bordered by the English Channel to the north. They emerged as a distinct group in the 10th century when the territory was granted to Viking leader Rollo in 911, leading to the establishment of a powerful duchy known for its seafaring skills and military prowess [1]. Today, Normandy is culturally significant, famous for its landscapes, historic sites, and its connection to the Norman Conquest of England in 1066, led by William the Conqueror, a Norman duke [1].

results.question: In what country is Normandy located?
results.answer: Normandy is indeed located in the northern part of France along the English Channel and is known for its rich history, including the D-Day landings during World War II. It is one of the 18 administrative regions of France and includes departments such as Calvados, Manche, and Seine-Maritime [1]. The region is famous for its cultural heritage, including Mont Saint-Michel and the historic city of Rouen, as well as its diverse geography, which features cliffs and lush pastures [1].

results.question: When were the Normans in Normandy?
results.answer:
The Normans emerged in Normandy in the early 10th century, specifically after the Treaty of Saint-Clair-sur-Epte in 911, when the Viking leader Rollo was granted land by the French king Charles the Simple. This event marked the establishment of Norman identity as a distinct cultural group, as they were descended from Norse raiders who assimilated with the local populations [1]. 

Over the following decades, the Normans solidified their power and influence in Normandy, with significant developments occurring throughout the 10th and 11th centuries. By the late 11th century, they had become a formidable force in Europe, culminating in the conquest of England in 1066 [1].

The Norman presence in Normandy continued until the end of the 13th century, after which the region became more integrated into the French kingdom. However, the legacy of the Normans persisted, influencing both French and English cultures significantly [2].

results.question: From which countries did the Norse originate?
results.answer: The Norse people primarily originated from the Scandinavian region, which includes modern-day Norway, Sweden, and Denmark, serving as the cultural and geographical heart of Norse civilization during the Viking Age [1]. Additionally, Norse explorers and raiders settled in Iceland and Greenland, further expanding their cultural influence [1]. While the majority of the Norse originated from Scandinavia, some groups migrated to regions such as the British Isles, including England, Scotland, and Ireland, contributing to the spread of Norse culture beyond their homeland [1].

results.question: What century did the Normans first gain their separate identity?
results.answer: The Normans first gained their separate identity in the 10th century as Viking settlers in Normandy began to adopt local culture and language, differentiating themselves from their Norse origins. This process was marked by the establishment of the Duchy of Normandy under the Viking leader Rollo, who was granted land by King Charles III of West Francia. The blending of Scandinavian roots with Frankish customs was crucial for the emergence of the Normans as a distinct group [1]. The development of a unique Norman identity continued throughout the 10th century, as the descendants of these settlers embraced Christianity and feudalism, leading to significant cultural and political changes [2].

results.question: Who ruled the duchy of Normandy
results.answer:
The duchy of Normandy was indeed originally ruled by Viking chieftain Rollo, who became the first Duke of Normandy after the treaty with King Charles the Simple in 911 [1]. Rollo's descendants continued to govern the duchy, establishing a powerful lineage that influenced both French and English history [1]. 

William the Conqueror, also known as William I of England, was a notable Duke of Normandy in the 11th century. He unified and expanded the duchy, and in 1066, he invaded England, claiming the English throne after his victory at the Battle of Hastings [1]. 

After William's death in 1087, the duchy was ruled by his descendants, including his sons William II and Henry I. The duchy maintained its semi-autonomous status within the French kingdom until it was lost to the French crown during the reign of King Philip II in the early 13th century [1].

results.question: What religion were the Normans
results.answer: The Normans were primarily Christians, adhering to the Roman Catholic Church, which significantly influenced their culture and governance, including their conquests and the establishment of churches and monasteries across their territories [1]. Their integration of local customs and traditions, influenced by the regions they settled in, contributed to the distinctive Norman identity during the medieval period [2]. After the Norman Conquest of England in 1066, they reinforced their Christian faith through the construction of cathedrals and the promotion of ecclesiastical authority, facilitating the spread of Christianity and the integration of Norman and Anglo-Saxon cultures [1].

results.question: What is the original meaning of the word Norman?
results.answer: The term "Norman" originates from the Old Norse "Northman," referring to Viking settlers from Scandinavia who invaded and settled in France during the 9th and 10th centuries. These Norsemen, under leader Rollo, assimilated with the local Frankish and Roman-Gaulish populations, leading to the establishment of Normandy and the emergence of a distinct cultural identity by the 10th century [1][2]. The term evolved to represent not only the descendants of these Vikings but also their significant influence on European history, particularly through the Norman Conquest of England in 1066 [1].

results.question: When was the Latin version of the word Norman first recorded?
results.answer: The Latin version of the word "Norman," specifically "Normannus," was indeed first recorded in historical texts during the 9th century, as it is associated with the Norse settlers in what is now Normandy, France [1]. The term "Normannus" was used in various Latin chronicles in the early 10th century to describe the Viking settlers who established control over the region, reflecting their assimilation into the local culture [1]. The earliest documented use of "Normannus" can be traced back to the writings of Dudo of Saint-Quentin around 1000 AD, where he chronicled the history of the Normans and referenced their origins and conquests [1].

results.question: When was the Duchy of Normandy founded?
results.answer: The Duchy of Normandy was indeed founded in 911 AD when the Viking leader Rollo entered into a treaty with King Charles the Simple, granting Rollo control over the territory that would become known as Normandy [1]. This agreement was significant for integrating Viking settlers into the local culture, leading to the formation of a distinct Norman identity, which emerged in the first half of the 10th century and evolved over the succeeding centuries [2]. Rollo's acceptance of Christianity and governance were pivotal in shaping the region's development and influence in medieval France [1].

results.question: Who did Rollo sign the treaty of Saint-Clair-sur-Epte with?
results.answer: Rollo signed the treaty of Saint-Clair-sur-Epte with Charles the Simple in 911, which granted him control over the territory of Normandy in exchange for his loyalty and protection against Viking raids. This agreement marked the formal recognition of Rollo as the ruler of the newly established Duchy of Normandy and was significant for stabilizing Charles's realm by aligning with the Viking leader [1].

results.question: What part of France were the Normans located?
results.answer: The Normans were indeed primarily located in Normandy, a region in northern France bordered by the English Channel, characterized by coastal cliffs and rolling hills [1]. They originated from Viking settlers, led by Rollo, who were granted land by the French king in the early 10th century [1]. Over time, these settlers assimilated into the local culture, forming a powerful duchy [1]. Normandy is also known for its cultural contributions, such as the Bayeux Tapestry, which illustrates events leading up to the Norman Conquest of England in 1066 [1].
